backing.locator: operations-research literature on supervisory span under interruption

claim: Realized human-supervisor-to-concurrent-AI-case ratios in production CX deployments will center near N*≈5 (range 3–8), not the ~20 vendors assume, under realistic intervention rates.

data.locator: MAP-Elites paper v8 derivation — parameter assumptions (α, m₀, intervention rate) explicitly marked ASSUMED, not calibrated

qualifier.scope: WAGER, not a graded forecast — no public referee exists (deployment ratios are private). Confidence in the mechanism HIGH; in the specific number, MIXED. Excluded from all calibration curves.

rebuttal.bestCounter: Vendors may be right where intervention rates are driven near zero by narrow task scoping.
rebuttal.changesOurMind: ≥3 documented production deployments sustaining >12 concurrent cases per human supervisor at stable quality — standing public disconfirmation bounty (terms in methodology/nstar-bounty-terms.md).

referee: none available — deployment ratios are private; the standing public disconfirmation bounty is the resolution mechanism
warrant: Queueing behavior of interrupt-driven supervision: at realistic intervention rates and switching costs, supervisor attention saturates far below vendor marketing ratios.
